summary: Excellent soundboard master of the March 24‑1990 show, featuring a smoking Loser and standout Tennessee Jed jam.
review: The Miller‑remastered SBD captures one of the high‑water marks of the spring‑90 tour – tight playing, soaring solos and an especially hot second set. Highlights like Loser, Desolation Row, Franklin’s Tower and a blistering Tennessee Jed jam are presented with crystal‑clear mix and extra bass presence, making this recording a go‑to version of the night.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (mostly 5‑star) praise both the performance and the Miller soundboard remix; recording quality is described as excellent and show quality as legendary.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller’s remastered SBD is praised as crisp, balanced and superior to the official Dozin' release; Phil’s bass is noticeably louder in the second set but overall mix is clean with no reported drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets are described as high‑octane; the first set builds strong momentum with tight renditions, while the second set delivers a peak‑hour jam marathon (Playing > UJB transition, Mind Left Body, etc.) that many reviewers call “the whole thing is great.”

band_member_comments.Phil: Frequently noted as louder in the mix and ‘dropping bombs’; his bass work is highlighted throughout both sets.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s (Weir) guitar is turned up and his vocal interjections (‘Come on, Georgie!’) are a talking point; his rhythm playing shines especially on Tennessee Jed.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drum > Space segment is praised as serious business, with both drummers delivering a powerful groove.
